method_atom: A two-phase continuum model in which a population of discrete inertial particles is coupled by size-dependent drag to a turbulent carrier fluid inside a gravitationally stratified medium, the turbulence being sustained by a shear-driven linear instability. The heavy phase sediments toward the midplane into an equilibrium Gaussian density profile whose vertical spread scales inversely with the square root of particle size, and the relative drift between the two coupled phases triggers a collective drag-driven aggregation (streaming) instability that spontaneously concentrates the heavy phase into elongated high-density filaments where converging flow streams intersect, sharply lowering the local random-velocity dispersion and the mean-free-path and collision times.
object_atom: Solid dust grains of a realistic size distribution embedded in the gas of a vertically stratified protostellar accretion disc with the density and temperature of a minimum-mass solar nebula, the gas driven to fully-developed turbulence by the magnetorotational instability, simulated at 10 au.
brief_reason: What carries across is the drag-coupled two-phase sedimentation-plus-relative-drift aggregation instability in a stratified turbulent fluid; what is domain-specific is the dust-grain/gas protostellar accretion disc with MRI-driven turbulence.
